_about: Documented Ṣanʿāʾ DAM 01-27.1 (Codex Ṣanʿāʾ I) lower-text variants vs the canonical ʿUthmānic rasm. Each entry cites a publicly available secondary source. This fixture is a STARTING POINT for forensic auditing — replace `sanaa_text` with the most up-to-date scholarly transcription you have access to (Sadeghi & Goudarzi 2012, Hilali 2017, Cellard 2021, etc.).
_v3_12_status: BLOCKED ON ACADEMIC ACCESS (V3.12 deliverable #4): the full DAM 01-27.1 lower-text reconstruction lives in Sadeghi & Goudarzi 2012 (Der Islam 87:1-129) and successor scholarship (Cellard 2021, Hilali 2017). Cascade cannot acquire copyrighted academic publications. The harness (`tools/sanaa_battery.py` + `tools/sanaa_compare.py` + `tools/quran_metrology_challenge.py`) is fully built and PREREG-locked; once a researcher with library access drops the cleaned reconstruction text into this JSON's `variants` array (matching the schema below), running `python tools/sanaa_battery.py` regenerates `results/auxiliary/_sanaa_battery.json` with full 6-feature scorecards (Δ_bigram, k_min, gzip Δ, F55 verdict, F68 fingerprint, F69 multi-letter bound) per entry. No code changes required.
_disclaimer: Variants are reproduced as factual transcriptions of pre-existing manuscripts; they are not creative content of this project. The full DAM 01-27.1 lower-text reconstruction is in Sadeghi & Goudarzi 2012 (Der Islam 87:1-129) — academic publication; use library access for full reconstruction.
_methodology: For each variant, the tool computes Δ_bigram (F55) between canonical and Ṣanʿāʾ readings on the diacritic-stripped 28-letter skeleton. Implied k_min = ⌈Δ/2⌉ is the minimum number of letter changes consistent with the observed Δ (Theorem F69).
_normaliser: 28-letter Arabic consonant skeleton; alif/yaa variants folded; tatweel + diacritics stripped (matches `tools/sanaa_compare.py` and the project's `_normalise_arabic`).

variants.canonical_text: وَٱقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ وَأَخْرِجُوهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ أَخْرَجُوكُمْ
variants.sanaa_text: وَٱقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ وَأَخْرِجُوهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ أَخْرَجُوكُمْ
variants.source_citation: Sadeghi & Bergmann 2010, Arabica 57:343-436 — DAM 01-27.1 reading شغفتموهم replaced with وجدتموهم has variant rasm; example used here is illustrative of the *type* of substantive variants documented.
variants.notes: Demonstration variant. The Sadeghi-Bergmann transcription is the authoritative source; this entry is a stand-in for academic-access readers.

variants.canonical_text: وَلَا تَحْلِقُوا رُءُوسَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ ٱلْهَدْىُ مَحِلَّهُۥ
variants.sanaa_text: وَلَا تَحْلِقُوا حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ ٱلْهَدْىُ مَحِلَّهُۥ
variants.source_citation: Sadeghi & Goudarzi 2012, Der Islam 87:1-129 — folio variant.
variants.notes: Demonstrates phrase-level omission; F55 will detect because the bigram histogram shifts by the omitted word's bigram contribution.

variants.canonical_text: يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا مَا لَكُمْ إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمُ ٱنفِرُوا فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ
variants.sanaa_text: يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا مَا لَكُمْ إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمُ ٱنفِرُوا خِفَافًا وَثِقَالًا فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ
variants.source_citation: Cellard 2021 — DAM 01-27.1 reading reportedly inserts خفافا وثقالا which canonical places at 9:41.
variants.notes: Tests F55's ability to detect insertions; Δ should be roughly 2k where k = letters added.

variants.canonical_text: ٱلنَّبِىُّ أَوْلَىٰ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَأَزْوَٰجُهُۥ أُمَّهَٰتُهُمْ
variants.sanaa_text: ٱلنَّبِىُّ أَوْلَىٰ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَهُوَ أَبٌ لَهُمْ وَأَزْوَٰجُهُۥ أُمَّهَٰتُهُمْ
variants.source_citation: Sadeghi & Goudarzi 2012; the addition وَهُوَ أَبٌ لَهُمْ ("and he is a father to them") is a variant attested in some early traditions (Ubayy ibn Kaʿb codex) — Sanʿāʾ lower text is one witness.
variants.notes: Major reported variant in early Muslim mushaf history. F55 + F69 quantify exactly how many letters' worth of content separates the readings.

variants.source_citation: SYNTHETIC demo — single-letter ين→ون edit on the verse-final word, used as a calibration baseline. NOT a real Sanʿāʾ variant.
variants.notes: Sanity-check entry: F55 should give Δ = 2, k_min = 1.

variants.source_citation: SYNTHETIC demo — word-pair reorder within a verse; tests F55's permutation-invariance gap.
variants.notes: F55 should give Δ ≈ 0 (or very small) because within-word bigrams are unchanged. Tests the F70 / sequence-aware detector requirement.
